title: OpenClaw should discard late subprocess stdout instead of crashing after a run ends

source.license_note: Localized ACBench reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62477.

task.summary: Repair the listener guard so late subprocess stdout arriving after a run ends does not crash the gateway.
task.instructions: Investigate why late subprocess stdout is still sent into the listener after the run has ended, repair the guard so those events are dropped safely, and preserve normal in-run processing.
task.acceptance_notes: Alternative fixes are allowed as long as late subprocess output stops crashing the runtime.

visible_context.issue_text: Subprocess stdout can still arrive after a run ends, and the gateway crashes when the listener processes that late output.
error_logs: subprocess stdout event arrived after run end
Unhandled promise rejection: Agent listener invoked outside active run
reproduction_steps: Run the unit tests from the repository root.
visible_context.notes: This fixture reuses the same guard bug with subprocess stdout framing.

code_fault.description: Late subprocess stdout is raised instead of skipped after the active run ends.

constraints.notes: This GitHub-derived reproduction is expected to be repaired in code, not by changing tests.

notes: Code-only localized reproduction of openclaw/openclaw issue #62477.
